paper_title: Decapod Fossils from Maksymivka Quarry: Description and Comparison

methods.description: Process for collecting fossil decapod specimens from the Middle Miocene (Badenian) coralgal reefs at Maksymivka quarry, Ukraine, specifically from intraframework crevices and burrows. Includes details on preservation and specimen housing, as well as acquisition of comparative material.

steps.description: Decapod specimens used in this paper were collected by one of us (O.S.) at the Maksymivka quarry.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fossil decapod specimens from a specific geological quarry site.
inputs: Maksymivka quarry location

steps.description: Specimens were collected in the intraframework crevices and burrows, filled by bioclastic sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fossil specimens from specific microhabitats (crevices, burrows) within the site.
inputs: Raw fossil decapod specimens (from step 1)
Intraframework crevices and burrows within coralgal buildups
Bioclastic sediment
outputs: Fossil decapod specimens from specific microhabitats

steps.description: Specimens were found as very well preserved disarticulated carapaces and isolated chelae, without signs of predation.
steps.searchable_summary: Document the preservation state of collected fossil specimens.
inputs: Collected fossil decapod specimens
outputs: Preservation notes/records (e.g., disarticulated carapaces, isolated chelae, preservation quality, absence of predation signs)

steps.description: Type specimens of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp. are housed in the Regional Museum of Ternopil (Ukraine) under acronym TKMP and Museu de Geologia de Barcelona-Museu de Ciències Naturals de Barcelona under acronym MGB.
steps.searchable_summary: Deposit type fossil specimens in designated museum collections.
inputs: Type fossil specimens of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.
outputs: Cataloged type specimens in museum collections (TKMP, MGB)

steps.description: Photographic material of extant Bathynectes species for comparison comes from the Museu Balear de Ciències Naturals of Sóller (Majorca, Balearic Islands) under acronym MBCN , Xavier Castellà (Badalona, Catalonia) private collection (XC), Pere Abelló (Barcelona) private collection (PA) and Antonio de Angeli (Verona, Italy) private collection (DA).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire comparative photographic data of extant species from various collections.
inputs: Collection contacts/locations (MBCN, XC, PA, DA)
outputs: Photographic data of extant Bathynectes species

steps.description: Other specimens figured herein under acronym AO, belong to the collection of one of the present authors (A.O.).
steps.searchable_summary: Document use of specimens from author's private collection.
inputs: Author's private collection (A.O.)

methods.description: Method used to compare the morphological characteristics of the new fossil decapod species (Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.) with extant and other fossil species, primarily focusing on dorsal carapace features and isolated chelae, using existing literature and keys, to establish taxonomic placement and species differentiation.

steps.description: Bathynectes muelleri n. sp. was originally compared to extant coral-inhabiting portunid crabs from subfamilies Carupinae Paul'son, 1875, Portuninae Rafinesque, 1815 and Thalamitinae Paul'son, 1875 (Spiridonov & Neumann, 2008). Bathynectes muelleri differs from all these groups by the shape and number of anterolateral teeth, the maximum width located in a different place of the carapace, a front with distinct orbito-frontal ratio or a different number of frontal teeth.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform morphological comparison of fossil species against extant subfamilies based on specified features.
inputs: Morphological data of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.
Morphological data/descriptions of extant portunid subfamilies (Carupinae, Portuninae, Thalamitinae)
outputs: Comparative morphological observations/differences relative to portunid subfamilies

steps.description: Bathynectes muelleri n. sp. was also compared with the representative genera of family Polybiidae Ortmann, 1893 (sensu Schubart & Reuschel, 2009) considering only the dorsal characters which we are dealing with, and the only one that could clearly accommodate the new species was Bathynectes Stimpson, 1871. Genera Liocarcinus Stimpson, 1871, Macropipus Prestandrea, 1833 and Ovalipes Rathbun, 1898 have a front with three lobes or teeth, whereas Bathynectes muelleri n. sp. has four; in addition, their carapace shape, ornamentation and number and form of anterolateral teeth are different than in Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform morphological comparison of fossil species against extant genera within a specific family (Polybiidae) based on dorsal characters.
inputs: Morphological data of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.
Morphological data/descriptions of Liocarcinus, Macropipus, Ovalipes
Taxonomic framework (sensu Schubart & Reuschel, 2009)
outputs: Comparative morphological observations/differences relative to Polybiidae genera
Justification for placement within genus Bathynectes

steps.description: Bathynectes muelleri n. sp. can be easily distinguished from the four extant species of the genus. Bathynectes longispina Stimpson, 1871 differs... Bathynectes maravigna (Prestandrea, 1839) differs... Bathynectes piperitus Manning & Holthuis, 1981 differs... The fourth species of the genus, Bathynectes longipes (Risso, 1816), is most similar to B. muelleri n. sp. in the relatively low four-lobed front and structure of the anterolateral margin. Bathynectes longipes differs from B. muelleri in having a very well-marked, complete and acute (mainly in young specimens) ridge that connects the two epibranchial teeth whereas in B. muelleri the swollen transverse ridge is less developed and is interrupted at the metagastric level. Bathynectes longipes also has an open medial supraorbital fissure wich is closed in B. muelleri... In addition, the two transverse ridges present on both protogastric lobes of B. longipes are absent in the new species.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform detailed morphological comparison of fossil species against extant species within the same genus (Bathynectes) based on specific features to identify distinguishing characteristics.
inputs: Morphological data of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.
Morphological data/descriptions/photographs of extant Bathynectes species (B. longispina, B. maravigna, B. piperitus, B. longipes)
Photographic material of extant species (from Collection method, step 5)
outputs: Detailed comparative morphological observations/differences relative to extant Bathynectes species
Basis for species differentiation of B. muelleri n. sp.

steps.description: When working on fossil crabs, the available characters that can be used in a diagnosis will clearly be less than with extant crabs. It depends a great deal on the degree of preservation of the fossils. ... reef associated fossil decapods frequently only have their carapaces and/or isolated chelae preserved. Other than isolated chelae that could be assigned to B. muelleri, which closely resembles that of B. longipes chelae... we have had no choice but to limit the comparison between the two species to the dorsal caparace characters.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter morphological comparison to only include characters available from fossil preservation (dorsal carapace, isolated chelae).
inputs: Full set of potential decapod morphological characters
Preservation state notes for fossil specimens (from Collection method, step 3)
outputs: Subset of morphological characters used for comparison (dorsal carapace, isolated chelae)

steps.description: Dorsal features of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp. match well with the diagnostic characters of the genus Bathynectes used by Stimpson (1871, p. 146)... and also with the useful generic diagnosis and specific keys provided by both Zariquiey (1968, pp. 380-383, figs. 127 c, f, g) for B. longipes and B. superbus (= B. maravigna) and Manning & Holthuis (1983, pp. 76-83) for B. piperitus and B. maravigna.
steps.searchable_summary: Utilize existing generic diagnoses and specific keys from published literature to guide morphological comparisons and taxonomic placement.
inputs: Morphological data of Bathynectes muelleri n. sp.
Published taxonomic keys and diagnoses for Bathynectes and related groups (Stimpson 1871, Zariquiey 1968, Manning & Holthuis 1983)
outputs: Confirmation of generic placement based on existing diagnoses
Confirmation of specific differences based on existing keys
